product_summary.one_sentence_summary: B2B expat onboarding package for Luxembourg that bundles language, civic integration and practical life-in-country support.
product_summary.target_user: HR teams and mobility managers relocating employees to Luxembourg, with the relocated employees as the end users.
product_summary.core_pain: Companies need faster, less fragile relocation and integration support so new hires can function socially and professionally in Luxembourg sooner.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Better relocation success, faster integration and lower turnover risk.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The natural buyer is the company relocating the employee.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: HR or mobility teams typically own onboarding and relocation support.
Relocation, language and integration partners are likely needed to deliver the bundle.

budget_owner.reason: Relocation and integration spend usually sits with HR or mobility budgets.

buyer_influencers.reason: Managers care whether the employee becomes productive quickly.
Relocation, language leave and residency-related questions often need legal or policy review.
SME leaders may influence whether relocation support is treated as a retention investment.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Luxembourg has a linguistic leave scheme of up to 200 hours for language courses.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Guichet states that linguistic leave exists and can be used for language learning under defined conditions.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: The citizen's pact for intercultural living together includes introductory modules for people living or working in Luxembourg.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The Biergerpakt page says signatories can access information and training modules about intercultural living together and everyday life in Luxembourg.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Luxembourg already has public and private substitutes for language and integration support.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: INLL offers public language learning and certification, Prolingua offers private language courses, and Guichet publishes civic integration modules.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Cross-border workers make up about 47% of Luxembourg's workforce.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: A 2025 EU labour mobility report says Luxembourg’s cross-border worker share is 45.1%, so the card’s 47% figure is directionally right but not exact.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: No one sells a single relocation language + integration package.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The public substitutes already combine language learning, civic integration and work/life-in-country support, so the absolute uniqueness claim is too strong.

internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No public source reviewed here confirmed the exact figure.
The public source reviewed gives a different share-based statistic rather than this exact count.
This is internal commercial math, not an externally verified result.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The product's language certificate or Sproochtest readiness evaluation has external acceptance value.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed public sources do not confirm that this new product’s deliverables have official recognition.
This is a market interpretation, not a public fact.

regulator_entities.name: Ministry of Labour
regulator_entities.relevance: Responsible for the linguistic leave scheme on Guichet.lu.

framework_entities.name: Linguistic leave
framework_entities.relevance: Public leave scheme enabling employees or self-employed people to learn a language in Luxembourg.

framework_entities.name: Citizen's pact for intercultural living together
framework_entities.relevance: Public civic-integration framework with modules about everyday life, access to information and participation in Luxembourg.

funding_bodies.name: INFPC / Guichet.lu
funding_bodies.relevance: Relevant if the product is sold as company training in Luxembourg.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Linguistic leave can be requested under defined conditions to learn a language.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Employees and self-employed people in Luxembourg

regulatory_requirements.requirement: The intercultural living-together programme offers modules about life in Luxembourg and can support long-term residence or nationality procedures under certain conditions.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: People living or working in Luxembourg

subsidies_or_funding.name: Linguistic leave
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: A worker-level entitlement rather than a company subsidy, but highly relevant to the product story.

subsidies_or_funding.name: Continuing vocational training co-funding
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: General company training co-funding exists; product-level eligibility for the bundle should be checked.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: INLL
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public language-learning and certification institution in Luxembourg.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Prolingua
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Private language school with French, German, Luxembourgish and other course offerings in Luxembourg.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Citizen's Pact / intercultural living-together modules
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public civic integration modules cover part of the same integration job.

legal_claim_severity.reason: This is mainly a training and reimbursement story, not a regulated-practice claim.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: The program can be delivered without formal approval, though localisation matters.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about support routes, language leave and integration outcomes should be checked carefully.

proof_stack.reason: The employer must accept the bundle as worth buying instead of using separate providers.
A pilot with one relocation cohort would validate the bundle quickly.
Participants should show better language and local-context understanding after the programme.

proof_burden.reason: Even though the offer is not regulated, it needs buyer acceptance and proof that the integration bundle is better than piecemeal alternatives.

dependency_types.reason: The whole product depends on Luxembourg-specific content and support routes.
The bundle requires language instructors capable of delivering relevant languages and levels.
The sales story partly depends on linguistic leave and possibly company training co-funding.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The current Luxembourg scope is coherent, but the product must fit very different employee backgrounds and employers.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: The bundle is highly Luxembourg-specific; other countries would change language, civic and mobility rules.

subsidy_exists.reason: Linguistic leave and public company training co-funding exist in Luxembourg.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The product is aligned with genuine language and integration support mechanisms, but the exact bundle still needs checking.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: The card mixes worker entitlement, company training co-funding and onboarding outcomes in a way that should not be treated as a single confirmed subsidy claim.

market_regulatory_context.reason: This is an onboarding and integration product rather than a regulated-practice or certification route.

claim_language_risk.reason: Language and integration claims should stay grounded because the public support routes are real but not the same as a guaranteed outcome.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: The bundle depends on local language and integration expertise rather than formal approval.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Public and private substitutes already cover language and civic integration components.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The card uses population and ROI numbers that are not precisely supported by the public sources reviewed.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: The product combines several public support mechanisms, but the card presents them too simply.

positioning_risk.reason: Without a sharper integration edge, the offer can become a generic language bundle.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: No one sells a single relocation language + integration package.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: Public substitutes already combine language learning, civic integration and life-in-Luxembourg support.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Cross-border workers make up 47% of Luxembourg workforce.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The public 2025 report gives 45.1%, so the card is directionally right but not exact.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Decide whether the product is mainly language, civic integration or relocation support.
Clarify which support route, if any, is used for the company-facing version.
Validate the retention and ROI claims before using them in sales copy.

sources.title: Citizen's pact for intercultural living together - 'Biergerpakt'
Learn languages in a pleasant and welcoming environment
Annual Report on Intra-EU Labour Mobility - 2025 edition
Financial aid for in-company continuing vocational training
